subject: GRB 251130A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 00:43:07 UT on 30 Nov 2025, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 251130A (trigger 786156192.430142 / 251130030).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 176.6, Dec = -60.4 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 11h 46m, -60d 23'), with a statistical uncertainty of 5.1 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 99.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn251130030/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn251130030.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn251130030/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn251130030.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2025/bn251130030/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn251130030.gif
